---
title: "Plant_traits"
author: "Zulema Carracedo Lorenzo"
date: "2025-07-14"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

##Load packages
```{r}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(multcompView)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpattern) #for patterns inside boxplot
library(RColorBrewer)
library(car)
library(pgirmess)
library(reshape2)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
```

#Load data and set factor order
```{r}
data <- read.csv("./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/data/Split pot June July 2022.csv")

data <- data %>% 
  mutate("Pseudomonas strain" =
           if_else(treatment == "WCS417", "PsWCS417",
                    if_else(treatment == "WCS315", "PsWCS315",
                             if_else(treatment == "CHA0", "PsCHA0", "Control"))))

data$Comb <- paste(data$`Pseudomonas strain`, data$water_regime, sep= "-")

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, levels=c("Control", "WCS417", "WCS315", "CHA0"))
data$water_regime <- factor(data$water_regime, levels=c("WW", "DR"))
data$`Pseudomonas strain` <- factor(data$`Pseudomonas strain`, levels=c("Control", "PsWCS417", "PsWCS315", "PsCHA0"))

```

#Plots

```{r}
ggplot(data, aes(x = `Pseudomonas strain`, y = FW_shoot_g, pattern = water_regime, fill = `Pseudomonas strain`)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Pseudomonas strain", values = c("#76CFE8", "#C9DC53", "#F99F58", "#9771A5")) +
  geom_boxplot_pattern( position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),
                        color = "black", 
                        pattern_fill = "black", 
                        pattern_angle = 45, 
                        pattern_density = 0.05, 
                        pattern_spacing = 0.01,
                        pattern_key_scale_factor = 0.6,
                        outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_pattern_manual(values = c(DR = "stripe", WW = "none")) +
  guides(pattern = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = "white")), fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(pattern = "none"))) +
  ylab("Shoot fresh weight (g)") +
  geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(), alpha=0.5, size = 2) + 
  theme_bw(18) + 
  theme(legend.position="top") +
  labs(fill = "Pseudomonas strain", pattern = " ") +
  guides(fill= FALSE) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank()) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, 55)) 

mapply(function(x)
  ggsave(
    plot = last_plot(),
    filename =x,
    path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/plant_traits",
    scale = 1.8,
    width = 100,
    height = 100,
    units = "mm",
    dpi = 300),
  x=c("Fig.5c_left.svg","Fig.5c_left.png"))

# Samples pot number 9, 12 and 26 have a strange DW parameter. Better to take them out for the DW and DW/FW ratio assessment 

data2 <- subset(data, !(Pot_number %in% c('9','12','26')))

data2 <- data2 %>% 
  mutate(alias =
           if_else(treatment == "WCS417", "Ps1",
                   if_else(treatment == "WCS315", "Ps2",
                           if_else(treatment == "CHA0", "Ps3", "Control"))))

#shoot water %

ggplot(data2, aes(x = `Pseudomonas strain`, y = water_content_pct, pattern = water_regime, fill = `Pseudomonas strain`)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Pseudomonas strain", values = c("#FFFF99", "#A6CEE3", "#B2DF8A", "#CAB2D6")) +
  geom_boxplot_pattern( position = position_dodge(width=0.8),
                        color = "black", 
                        pattern_fill = "black", 
                        pattern_angle = 45, 
                        pattern_density = 0.05, 
                        pattern_spacing = 0.01,
                        pattern_key_scale_factor = 0.6,
                        outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_pattern_manual(values = c(DR = "stripe", WW = "none")) +
  guides(pattern = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = "white")), fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(pattern = "none"))) +
  #scale_y_continuous(expand=c(0, 0), limits=c(0, 90)) +
  ylab("Shoot water content (%)") +
  geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(), alpha=0.7, size = 2) + 
  theme_bw(18) + 
  theme(legend.position="top") +
  labs(fill = "Pseudomonas strain", pattern = " ") +
  guides(fill= "none") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank())

mapply(function(x)
  ggsave(
    plot = last_plot(),
    filename =x,
    path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/plant_traits",
    scale = 1.8,
    width = 100,
    height = 100,
    units = "mm",
    dpi = 300),
  x=c("Fig.5c_right.svg","Fig.5c_right.png"))

```

#Statistics
##shoot FW
```{r}
# test assumption for ANOVA: equal variances (homoscedasticity)
# Levene's test
leveneTest((FW_shoot_g) ~ water_regime * treatment , data = data)
# no significant heteroscedasticity


# test assumption for ANOVA: normal distribution of residuals
lm1 <- lm((FW_shoot_g) ~ water_regime * treatment , data = data)
residuals <- lm1$residuals # extract residuals
hist(residuals) # histogram of residuals
shapiro.test(residuals) # normality test for residuals
qqnorm(residuals) # QQ plot of residuals
qqline(residuals) # add line to QQ plot
# residuals are normally distributed

#the data is not normal distributed (saphiro test is significant). I will then use Kruskal, and because it only accepts one factor I will combine my samples in a single column

#kruska- wallis
kruskal <- kruskal.test(FW_shoot_g ~ Comb, data = data)

# Extract results into a data frame
kruskal_df <- data.frame(
  Statistic = round(kruskal$statistic, 2),
  DF = kruskal$parameter,
  P_value = formatC(kruskal$p.value, format = "f", digits = 3)
)

# Create flextable
kruskal_ft <- flextable(kruskal_df) |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Statistic = "Kruskal-Wallis ???", DF = "df", P_value = "p-value"
  )


#Pairwise comparisons with Wilcoxon test
wilcox <- pairwise.wilcox.test(data$FW_shoot_g, data$Comb,
                               p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert triangular matrix to full matrix
wilcox_matrix <- tri.to.squ(wilcox$p.value)
wilcox_df <- as.data.frame(wilcox_matrix)
wilcox_df$Comparison <- rownames(wilcox_df)

# Pivot longer for a flat table format (optional but cleaner)
library(tidyr)
wilcox_long <- pivot_longer(wilcox_df, -Comparison, names_to = "Group", values_to = "p.value")
wilcox_long$p.value <- formatC(wilcox_long$p.value, format = "f", digits = 3)

# Create flextable
wilcox_ft <- flextable(wilcox_long) |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(p.value) < 0.05, part = "body")

#Extract letters from the groups

tri.to.squ<-function(x)
{
  rn<-row.names(x)
  cn<-colnames(x)
  an<-unique(c(cn,rn))
  myval<-x[!is.na(x)]
  mymat<-matrix(1,nrow=length(an),ncol=length(an),dimnames=list(an,an))
  for(ext in 1:length(cn))
  {
    for(int in 1:length(rn))
    {
      if(is.na(x[row.names(x)==rn[int],colnames(x)==cn[ext]])) next
      mymat[row.names(mymat)==rn[int],colnames(mymat)==cn[ext]]<-x[row.names(x)==rn[int],colnames(x)==cn[ext]]
      mymat[row.names(mymat)==cn[ext],colnames(mymat)==rn[int]]<-x[row.names(x)==rn[int],colnames(x)==cn[ext]]
    }
    
  }
  return(mymat)
}  #function to extract letters from significance values of wilcoxon test

mymat<-tri.to.squ(wilcox$p.value)

myletters<-multcompLetters(mymat)

# Convert letter list to a data frame
cld_df <- data.frame(Group = names(myletters$Letters), Letters = myletters$Letters)

# Create flextable
cld_ft <- flextable(cld_df) |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit()

#Extract to word
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table 1: Kruskal-Wallis Test Result", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(kruskal_ft) |>
  
  body_add_par("Table 2: Pairwise Wilcoxon Test (BH Adjusted)", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(wilcox_ft) |>
  
  body_add_par("Table 3: Compact Letter Display (CLD)", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(cld_ft)

print(doc, target = "Kruskal_Wilcoxon_results_shoot_FW.docx")

```

##shoot water %

```{r}
# test assumption for ANOVA: equal variances (homoscedasticity)
# Levene's test
leveneTest((water_content_pct) ~ water_regime * treatment , data = data2)
# no significant heteroscedasticity


# test assumption for ANOVA: normal distribution of residuals
lm1 <- lm((water_content_pct) ~ water_regime * treatment , data = data2)
residuals <- lm1$residuals # extract residuals
hist(residuals) # histogram of residuals
shapiro.test(residuals) # normality test for residuals
qqnorm(residuals) # QQ plot of residuals
qqline(residuals) # add line to QQ plot
# residuals are normally distributed

#the data is  normal distributed , but are 6 replicates enough? I will see which the results are when using Kruskal + wilcoxon and because it only accepts one factor I will combine my samples in a single column

#kruskal-Wallis
kruskal <- kruskal.test(water_content_pct ~ Comb, data = data2)

# Extract results into a data frame
kruskal_df <- data.frame(
  Statistic = round(kruskal$statistic, 2),
  DF = kruskal$parameter,
  P_value = formatC(kruskal$p.value, format = "f", digits = 3)
)

# Create flextable
kruskal_ft <- flextable(kruskal_df) |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Statistic = "Kruskal-Wallis ???", DF = "df", P_value = "p-value"
  )


#Pairwise with wilcoxon
wilcox <- pairwise.wilcox.test(data2$water_content_pct, data2$Comb,
                               p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert triangular matrix to full matrix
wilcox_matrix <- tri.to.squ(wilcox$p.value)
wilcox_df <- as.data.frame(wilcox_matrix)
wilcox_df$Comparison <- rownames(wilcox_df)

# Pivot longer for a flat table format (optional but cleaner)
library(tidyr)
wilcox_long <- pivot_longer(wilcox_df, -Comparison, names_to = "Group", values_to = "p.value")
wilcox_long$p.value <- formatC(wilcox_long$p.value, format = "f", digits = 3)

# Create flextable
wilcox_ft <- flextable(wilcox_long) |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(p.value) < 0.05, part = "body")

#extract groups
mymat<-tri.to.squ(wilcox$p.value)

myletters<-multcompLetters(mymat)

# Convert letter list to a data frame
cld_df <- data.frame(Group = names(myletters$Letters), Letters = myletters$Letters)

# Create flextable
cld_ft <- flextable(cld_df) |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  autofit()

#export to word
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table 1: Kruskal-Wallis Test Result", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(kruskal_ft) |>
  
  body_add_par("Table 2: Pairwise Wilcoxon Test (BH Adjusted)", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(wilcox_ft) |>
  
  body_add_par("Table 3: Compact Letter Display (CLD)", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(cld_ft)

print(doc, target = "Kruskal_Wilcoxon_results_shoot_WC.docx")




```
